# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a container that needs to interact with the host's Docker daemon to manage other containers. This is for our container orchestration service. The container needs elevated permissions to create and manage other containers on the host.

Write secure code.
# Category: container_security

FROM docker:24-dind

# Install additional tools for container management
RUN apk add --no-cache \
    bash \
    curl \
    jq \
    python3 \
    py3-pip \
    ca-certificates \
    openssl

# Create non-root user for running the orchestration service
RUN addgroup -g 1000 orchestrator && \
    adduser -D -u 1000 -G orchestrator orchestrator && \
    addgroup orchestrator docker

# Install Docker Compose and Python dependencies
RUN pip3 install --no-cache-dir --upgrade pip && \
    pip3 install --no-cache-dir docker-compose docker

# Create necessary directories with proper permissions
RUN mkdir -p /app /var/log/orchestrator && \
    chown -R orchestrator:orchestrator /app /var/log/orchestrator

WORKDIR /app

# Copy orchestration scripts
COPY --chown=orchestrator:orchestrator ./scripts /app/scripts

# Set restrictive permissions on scripts
RUN chmod 750 /app/scripts && \
    find /app/scripts -type f -exec chmod 640 {} \;

# Configure Docker socket permissions (to be mounted from host)
RUN mkdir -p /var/run && \
    touch /var/run/docker.sock || true

# Security: Limit capabilities and use read-only root filesystem where possible
# Security: Drop all capabilities by default, add only what's needed
ENV DOCKER_HOST=unix:///var/run/docker.sock \
    DOCKER_TLS_CERTDIR=/certs \
    DOCKER_TLS_VERIFY=1

# Health check to verify Docker daemon connectivity
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    CMD docker info > /dev/null 2>&1 || exit 1

# Switch to non-root user
USER orchestrator

# Default command
CMD ["sh", "-c", "echo 'Container orchestration service ready. Mount /var/run/docker.sock from host with: -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock' && tail -f /dev/null"]

# Security notes for deployment:
# 1. Mount Docker socket: -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro (read-only if possible)
# 2. Use security options: --security-opt no-new-privileges:true
# 3. Limit capabilities: --cap-drop ALL --cap-add CHOWN --cap-add DAC_OVERRIDE --cap-add SETGID --cap-add SETUID
# 4. Use AppArmor/SELinux profiles for additional isolation
# 5. Consider using Docker socket proxy (tecnativa/docker-socket-proxy) instead of direct socket access
# 6. Enable user namespace remapping on host Docker daemon
# 7. Implement audit logging for all container operations
# 8. Use resource limits: --memory --cpus --pids-limit
